title: Store downloaded AI model under the app support directory
description: ModelDownloader.getModelPath walks up from Application Support and appends Data/Library/Application Support/Flare. In non-sandbox builds this points under ~/Data/... instead of ~/Library/Application Support/Flare; in sandbox-style paths it can duplicate Data. The Settings model status and LLM load path depend on this computed path.
acceptance_criteria: Downloaded model path is derived from FileManager.applicationSupportDirectory/Flare consistently with PersistenceService, works for sandboxed and non-sandboxed builds, and any old misplaced model is migrated or detected with a clear recovery path.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Persist per-source refresh results even when no new jobs are found
description: JobManager.fetchJobsFromSource updates jobsBySource, then only calls processNewJobs when newJobs is non-empty. Timer refreshes that find zero new IDs do not recompute allJobs, update fetch statistics, save changed jobs, or remove disappeared postings.
acceptance_criteria: A per-source timer refresh updates allJobs, fetchStatistics, lastFetchTime, and persisted jobs for that source even when newJobs is empty. Notifications are still sent only for genuinely new or bumped jobs.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Tie custom board monitor lifecycle to global monitoring state
description: JobManager.startMonitoring starts JobBoardMonitor when custom boards are enabled, but JobManager.stopMonitoring only invalidates built-in source timers. JobBoardMonitor keeps its own 30-minute timer running, and enableCustomBoards changes are not part of sourceBindings.
acceptance_criteria: Stopping monitoring invalidates custom board monitoring too. Saving settings with custom boards disabled stops the custom-board timer; enabling it starts the timer only when monitoring is active. Custom board fetch interval uses the same configured refresh interval or a clearly separate setting.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Honor Include Remote Jobs setting across fetchers
description: Settings exposes includeRemoteJobs and says it automatically adds remote to location searches, but the fetchers currently add remote unconditionally or hard-code includeRemote=1. Turning the toggle off does not actually exclude remote searches.
acceptance_criteria: Remote augmentation is controlled by JobManager.includeRemoteJobs. When disabled, fetchers do not add remote/anywhere keywords or includeRemote=1 unless the user explicitly typed a remote keyword. Behavior is covered for Microsoft, Google, Greenhouse, Lever, iCIMS, Taleo, and custom-board fetch paths.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Do not invoke LLM when direct universal extraction already found jobs
description: UniversalJobFetcher calls tryLLMExtraction whenever AI is enabled and JSON/API extraction found jobs, just to compare counts. This spends a full model pass on successful simple/direct routes.
acceptance_criteria: If universal JSON/API extraction returns matching jobs, the fetcher returns them without invoking LLM. LLM is only used when direct extraction finds no jobs or explicit fallback discovery is needed.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Let LLMParser idle-unload manage model lifetime
description: SmartJobParser manually calls unloadModel after successful and failed branches even though LLMParser acquire/release already schedules idle unload. This can reload the 3B model repeatedly across boards in one refresh.
acceptance_criteria: SmartJobParser no longer force-unloads after parser calls; model remains warm across nearby LLM operations and unloads through LLMParser's idle timer.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Cache simple API extraction routes separately from HTML extraction
description: Simple API extraction success is cached by setting htmlExtractionWorks, so later refreshes render WebKit again to rediscover the API route instead of fetching the known endpoint directly. This makes existing autodetected API routes look like full refetches in logs.
acceptance_criteria: When a simple API endpoint is known, refresh attempts direct cached endpoint extraction before WebKit rendering; WebKit is only used if the cached endpoint fails.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Use stable IDs for AI, HTML, and schema-extracted jobs
description: UUID-based IDs in SmartJobParser, JobBoardMonitor quick extraction, and UniversalJobFetcher cause the same extracted posting to be treated as new on every refresh. Use deterministic IDs from provider ID when present, otherwise canonical job URL/title/company/location hash.
acceptance_criteria: Repeated fetches of the same AI/HTML/schema extracted board produce the same job IDs; no duplicate persisted jobs or repeated new-job notifications for unchanged postings.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Wire iCIMS and Taleo through AI-detected ATS fetch paths
description: Direct board detection can preview iCIMS/Taleo, but SmartJobParser.fetchFromDetectedATS and JobBoardMonitor cached detectedATS refresh dispatch only Workday, Greenhouse, Lever, and Ashby.
acceptance_criteria: iCIMS and Taleo detected via script/WebKit/LLM paths can fetch immediately and continue fetching after detectedATSURL/detectedATSType are persisted.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Open Settings tab on first launch
description: First launch set JobManager.showSettings, but no view observed that property, so the app still opened on the Jobs tab.
acceptance_criteria: First launch selects the Settings tab directly and no dead showSettings state remains.

title: Refresh job list when applied or starred state changes
description: JobListView recalculates filtered rows on search/source/status-toggle/allJobs changes, but not when JobManager.appliedJobIds or starredJobIds change. With the Starred or Applied filter enabled, toggling a row/detail action can leave the list showing stale rows until another filter change or refresh occurs.
acceptance_criteria: JobListView observes appliedJobIds and starredJobIds changes and recomputes the filtered list while preserving pagination sensibly. Row badges and filtered membership update immediately after star/apply actions.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Register intended first-run defaults for built-in sources
description: Built-in source toggles use UserDefaults.bool(forKey:), which defaults to false when unset. SettingsView state initializes several sources to true, but loadSettings immediately overwrites those values from JobManager, so fresh installs can start with all built-in sources disabled.
acceptance_criteria: App startup registers explicit defaults for built-in sources and settings. First launch shows and uses the intended enabled source set consistently across JobManager and SettingsView.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Validate custom board URLs before detection and import
description: The Add Board form treats URL(string:) != nil as valid, and JobBoardConfig.init does not reject malformed or unsupported URLs. Strings without an http(s) scheme/host can pass validation and later fail detection or cache under unusable values.
acceptance_criteria: Custom board add/import paths only accept http or https URLs with a host, normalize whitespace and scheme casing, and surface a user-facing validation error before detection. Existing valid configs continue to load.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Load persisted job state independently and tolerate missing files
description: JobManager.loadStoredData wraps jobs, stored IDs, applied IDs, and starred IDs in one do/catch. PersistenceService.loadJobs, loadStoredJobIds, and loadAppliedJobIds throw when files are missing, so a fresh install or one missing/corrupt file can prevent unrelated state from loading.
acceptance_criteria: Missing jobs/stored/applied/starred files are treated as empty. Each persisted state file loads independently so failure in one file does not prevent the others from loading. Corrupt-file errors remain visible in logs or UI diagnostics.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Reschedule active fetch timers when refresh interval changes
description: refreshInterval is persisted on change, but already scheduled Timer instances keep their original interval. Settings.saveSettings calls startMonitoring, which reschedules built-in source timers, but direct property changes and JobBoardMonitor's separate hard-coded timer still do not consistently follow the new interval.
acceptance_criteria: Changing refreshInterval reschedules all active built-in source timers and the custom-board timer without requiring app restart. Invalid or tiny values are clamped to a safe minimum. Existing monitoring state is preserved.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Avoid returning unfiltered AI/WebKit extraction results when filters remove all jobs
description: SmartJobParser returns unfiltered jobs when title/location filters remove every extracted result. That can make custom board refresh ignore user filters and import noisy boards.
acceptance_criteria: Fallback extraction respects filters consistently; if filters remove all jobs, the parser reports no matching jobs instead of returning the unfiltered set.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Improve non-LLM JSON extraction before schema discovery
description: trySimpleAPIExtraction only checks top-level arrays and a few top-level keys. Many career APIs nest jobs under data.search.results, props.pageProps, nodes, edges, or similar containers, forcing unnecessary LLM schema discovery.
acceptance_criteria: Parser can find likely job dictionaries recursively within bounded JSON depth and extract jobs without LLM when title/url/id/location fields are present.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Replay intercepted WebKit API requests more faithfully
description: Simple API extraction currently requires absolute URLs and loses POST request bodies. Relative intercepted endpoints and POST/GraphQL searches can be dropped or replayed as empty requests.
acceptance_criteria: Simple extraction resolves relative API URLs against the page URL, preserves request method/body/content-type where available, and skips non-JSON/non-job endpoints cheaply.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse
